final_explanation:
Based on the AI model's prediction,
The fact that there is no checking account due to its lower association with default probability, because of this lower association, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk.[1]
The fact that the checking account balance is below zero as standard models rely on broader financial ratios, because of this reliance, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk.[2]
The fact that the savings account balance is low because tree-based frameworks use general stability indicators, because of this indicator use, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that decreases risk.[3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
